regime_justification: USDJPY near 160 watch-line with BOJ rate hike priced for next week; intervention threat renewed but spread remains wide; oil easing tempers imported inflation.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 160.19, within pips of MOF red line; BOJ June 15-16 hike 49/51 priced; carry spread 182 bps still elevated but rate convergence underway.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ June 16 decision (25bp hike consensus) and oil peace rumors lowering ¥/bbl pressure keep near-term unwind risk contained but elevated.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ reaction function unknown; if hike+hawkish guidance, spread compression accelerates; if dovish, MOF intervention risk fades and carry extends.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Scenario-weighted: Iran peace deal probability (80% per officials) could anchor WTI sub-$85, relieving ¥ pressure; alternatively, deal collapse reignites energy shock.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Base case: BOJ normalizes gradually, oil stabilizes, USDJPY drifts to 155-158 range; carry unwind risk declines unless Fed rhetoric turns dovish or Hormuz re-closes.

bottom_line: Stage-2 pre-intervention setup: USDJPY at 160, BOJ hike 90% priced for Monday, oil falling on Iran peace hopes. Carry spread still 182 bps but convergence narrative gaining traction. Grades unchanged at C/C/C/D—watchful but not imminent. Key risk: hawkish BOJ surprise or Iran deal collapse.

stack.note: 18 pips below 160; MOF watch-line in play
Down on Iran peace hopes; easing rate pressure
Off May 2.8% peak; following UST lower
Carry still intact but compressing from 200+ highs
Down 6% on week; Trump-Iran deal 80% probability
Easing import cost pressure vs ¥16,000+ peaks
Elevated but below panic; equity vol modest
Strong bounce Friday on oil relief; decoupling from USDJPY stress
Stabilizing mid-$60k; liquidity tepid, no risk-on signal yet
49/51 economists expect 25bp hike to 1.0%; market fully priced

trigger_map.note: USDJPY at 160.19, near May intervention level (¥160); verbal warnings active but no rate-check rumors yet; could arm to 'cocked' if USDJPY prints 160.50+.
June 16 decision: consensus 0.75%→1.0% (25bp); already priced. Trigger only fires if 50bp or explicit H2 2026 guidance shocks market.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: Currently 2.64%; May peak was 2.8% (29yr high). Watching but not imminent; spread compression more relevant than outright JGB level.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl now ¥13,600 vs ¥16,000+ May peak; Iran peace deal 80% probable per officials. If deal collapses, trigger flips to 'cocked' within 48hrs.
MOVE data unavailable but UST vol muted; 10Y down 6bp quietly. No rates-vol regime change signaled.

narrative.p1: USDJPY closed Friday at 160.19, inching back toward the MOF intervention watch-line after consolidating mid-week. Oil collapsed 3.2% on Trump-Iran peace hopes (officials cite 80% deal probability by weekend), pushing WTI to $84.88 and relieving ¥/bbl import cost pressure to ¥13,600 from May's ¥16,000+ peak. Nikkei rallied 2.8% on the oil relief, decoupling from FX stress. US 10Y fell 6bp to 4.47%, JGB 10Y down 5bp to 2.64%; spread compressed 1bp to 183bps but remains historically wide. BOJ June 15-16 meeting now 3 days out with 49/51 economists expecting 25bp hike to 1.0%—market fully priced. No material change from yesterday's yellow regime, but oil decline is the key incremental positive.
narrative.p2: Carry spread at 183bps is 17bps tighter than the 200bp peaks but still supports the trade structurally. USDJPY 5-day change is +0.15%, flat—pace has decelerated from the sharp May ramp that triggered intervention. Distance from 60d mean is slightly stretched (qualitative: ~2% above), but volatility remains subdued. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M implied vol data not retrieved). We remain in Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at the watch-line, BOJ decision imminent, but no rate-checks or panic vol. Grades hold at C across 1-3 weeks: meaningful stress building, setup fragile, but no trigger firing yet. The 4-week grade stays D as base case assumes gradual BOJ normalization and stable oil post-Iran deal. No application of unwind prior—data is genuinely mixed (oil easing vs USDJPY at 160), so grades reflect the balance.
narrative.p3: BTC at $63,800 (+0.4% on the day, +2.1% in 24hr per some sources) is stabilizing but lacks conviction. Volume tepid, no clear risk-on rotation signal. The modest bounce mirrors equity relief (Nikkei +2.8%) but crypto has not decoupled from macro—DXY and rates still drive. XRP data unavailable, BTC open interest direction and BTC-DXY correlation also missing, so liquidity sympathy read is incomplete. However, the directional takeaway is unchanged: crypto remains a hostage to carry unwind risk and DXY strength. If BOJ delivers hawkish hike Monday and USDJPY compresses to 155, BTC could leg up on liquidity relief; if BOJ disappoints or Iran deal collapses, expect further crypto drainage. For now, BTC is a hold-and-watch—no actionable rotation signal yet.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-week = C (watchful): BOJ decision June 16 is the live catalyst. Consensus 25bp hike is priced, so the risk is binary—hawkish guidance (spread compresses faster, grade improves to D) vs dovish hold/hike (MOF intervention risk fades, spreads widen, grade holds C). Oil at $84.88 is a tailwind but fragile pending Iran deal confirmation. 2-week = C (watchful): Post-BOJ reaction function dominates. If hike + forward guidance, spread compression accelerates and we drift toward Stage 1. If dovish, carry extends and we revert to Stage 1 (pressure building) rather than Stage 2. Either way, volatility likely picks up post-decision, keeping grade at C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2-week = C, 3-week = C (both watchful): The Iran peace deal is the swing variable. Officials cite 80% probability, but geo risk is binary. If deal signed by G7 meeting (likely June 21-22 window), oil stays sub-$85, ¥/bbl pressure dissipates, and USDJPY drifts lower even without MOF action—grade could ease to D by week 3. If deal collapses (20% tail), WTI spikes back above $100, ¥/bbl surges past ¥16,000, and MOF is forced to intervene—grade flips to B (likely unwind). Scenario-weighting: 80% benign path (grade D by week 3) vs 20% shock path (grade B). Blended = C, tilted cautious given proximity to 160 and BOJ uncertainty.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3-week = C (watchful), 4-week = D (unlikely): Base case for week 4 assumes BOJ hikes in steps (June +25bp, October next move per former official), oil stabilizes post-Iran deal, and USDJPY drifts to 155-158 range as spread narrows gradually. Carry unwind risk declines materially in this scenario. The grade steps down to D because the acute triggers (intervention, BOJ shock, energy spike) will have resolved or de-risked by then. Tail risk: Fed turns dovish (unlikely given May CPI 4.2% 3yr high), or Hormuz re-closes (20% probability). Either tail flips the grade back to C or B, but neither is base case.

watch_list: BOJ June 16 decision statement and Ueda press conference: any explicit H2 2026 rate path guidance will move USDJPY ±200 pips.
Iran-US peace deal signing (G7 window June 21-22): confirmation anchors WTI sub-$85 and removes energy tail risk; collapse sends oil to $100+ and forces MOF action.
MOF Mimura or Katayama verbal intervention escalation: rate-checks or 'we will act without delay' phrasing shifts trigger from 'armed' to 'cocked'.
USDJPY daily close above 160.50: historically triggers MOF action within 24-48 hours based on May precedent.
US May PPI/CPI revisions or Fed June dots (June 18 FOMC): any dovish tilt widens US-JP spread again and re-prices carry higher.
